requirements.statement: Every user-facing natural-language string shall use a stable semantic locale key rather than being assembled from untranslated fragments.
requirements.acceptance: The localization audit finds no unapproved natural-language strings in production UI source and all named placeholders are preserved across catalogs.

requirements.statement: Locale catalogs shall be UTF-8, NFC-normalized, schema-valid, and complete relative to the en-IE source catalog.
requirements.acceptance: Malformed Unicode, missing keys, extra keys, blank messages, HTML, and placeholder drift fail the localization workflow.

requirements.statement: The interface shall tolerate pseudolocalized expansion, reflow, and language-specific line breaking without clipping, inaccessible focus, or horizontal overflow.
requirements.acceptance: The en-XA suite passes at 320 and 390 CSS pixels and at 400% zoom and reflow for the supported desktop workflows.

requirements.statement: A non-source locale shall not be marked release-ready without named, dated, locale-appropriate human review and accessibility sampling.
requirements.acceptance: The validator rejects release-ready catalogs without review evidence and release evidence identifies reviewer, scope, screenshots, limitations, and source commit.

requirements.statement: Localized accessibility names, descriptions, live messages, errors, and recovery actions shall communicate the same action and state as visible text.
requirements.acceptance: A screen-reader sample finds no source-language accessibility text in a release-ready localized workflow.

requirements.statement: Dates, times, names, numbers, lists, units, and currencies shall use locale-aware formatting rather than string concatenation.
requirements.acceptance: Formatter tests cover en-IE, ga-IE, ja-JP, and pt-BR with no invented date order or decimal convention.

requirements.statement: Plugin locale catalogs shall be namespaced and shall not replace core locale keys without an explicit compatible extension contract.
requirements.acceptance: Plugin validation rejects an ungranted core-key override and preserves unknown plugin text when a plugin is disabled.

requirements.statement: Public localized metadata shall agree with the visible page language, while local relationship records shall not receive marketing metadata.
requirements.acceptance: Public-page tests reconcile hreflang, inLanguage, canonical URL, Open Graph, JSON-LD, and visible locale; vault fixtures contain none of that metadata by default.

uat.title: Use a pseudolocalized workflow without layout loss
uat.given: The desktop interface is running with the en-XA test catalog at 390 CSS pixels and 400% zoom and reflow.
uat.when: The user creates a workspace, opens People, submits a validation error, and returns to the previous field.
uat.then: Text wraps without clipping or horizontal overflow, focus remains visible, and every action and error has an accessible name.

uat.title: Reject an unreviewed locale release claim
uat.given: A ga-IE catalog contains every key but has no named fluent reviewer or review date.
uat.when: A maintainer attempts to mark the catalog release-ready.
uat.then: Validation fails and identifies the missing human, layout, and accessibility evidence without publishing the locale.

uat.title: Keep plugin locale keys within their namespace
uat.given: A plugin catalog defines its own keys and attempts to replace a core Save label.
uat.then: The namespaced keys are accepted, the core override is rejected, and no canonical profile value is removed.
